1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	
5	## Project Overview
6	
7	libnn is the Andes Neural Network Library — a C library of optimized NN operators for RISC-V and NDS32 architectures. It provides multiple implementation variants per function: plain C (portable reference), DSP-optimized (RISC-V P-extension), and Vector-optimized (RVV 0.8 and 1.0). It maintains a CMSIS-NN compatibility layer for ARM portability.
8	
9	## Optimization Focus
10	
11	Primary target: **AX45MPV FPGA board** with **VLEN=1024**. FPGA cycle counts are the ground truth for performance. QEMU is for correctness verification only. When optimizing, always validate at VLEN=1024 and consider VLEN=512/256 for portability but do not sacrifice VLEN=1024 performance for smaller VLENs.
12	
13	## Build Commands
14	
15	```bash
16	# Build the library for a specific CPU target
17	./build.sh <CPU_NAME> [EXTRA_FLAGS] [COMPILER] [OUTPUT_PATH]
18	
19	# Examples:
20	./build.sh D25                          # RV32 with DSP
21	./build.sh NX27V                        # RV64 with vector extension
22	./build.sh AX45MPV                      # RV64 with vector, 45-series tuning
23	./build.sh x64                          # Native x86-64 build (for host testing)
24	
25	# Supported CPUs: N10, D10, D15, N225, D23, N25, D25, NX25, AX25, A27, AX27,
26	#   N45, D45, NX45, AX45, A46MP, AX46MP, AX65, AX66, NX27V, AX45MPV, A46MPV, AX46MPV, x86, x64
27	```
28	
29	Output: `libnn.a` static library + `libnn.objdump` disassembly.
30	
31	## Testing
32	
33	```bash
34	# Run tests from a separate build directory:
35	mkdir build_dir && cd build_dir
36	
37	# Plain C algorithm with GDB simulator
38	../test.sh "" nds32le-elf-gcc nds32le-elf-run GDB BS3
39	
40	# DSP algorithm with RV32 simulator
41	../test.sh "" riscv32-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp"
42	
43	# Vector extension (RVV 1.0) with RV64 simulator
44	../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
45	
46	# DAILY mode (includes code coverage via gcov)
47	../test.sh "" <compiler> <simulator> <target> DAILY [flags]
48	```
49	
50	Unit tests are in `Examples/unit_func/t_*.c` (~301 test files). Each test uses pre-computed golden data from `Examples/unit_func/bin/`.
51	
52	### QEMU Validation (preferred for Claude Code)
53	
54	**IMPORTANT:** In Claude Code's sandbox, Andes QEMU's semihosting output goes to fd 9 instead of stdout. The standard `project/ax45mpv/run.sh` and QEMU wrapper scripts will produce zero output and report 0% pass rate. Always use the `qemu-validate/` scripts with `--strace` mode instead.
55	
56	```bash
57	# Build libnn
58	/usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/build_libnn.sh
59	
60	# Run a single test (always use --strace in Claude Code)
61	/usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/run_qemu_test.sh <test_name> --strace --vlen 1024
62	
63	# Examples:
64	/usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --strace --vlen 1024
65	/usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/run_qemu_test.sh relu_s8 --strace --vlen 512
66	```
67	
68	The `--strace` mode uses strace to capture semihosting writes and parses "accuracy checking pass/fail" from the trace. Tests can run in parallel for independent functions.
69	
70	```bash
71	# Run the full test suite (16 parallel jobs, VLEN=1024)
72	/usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/run_all_tests.sh 16 1024
73	```
74	
75	### Known Pre-existing Test Failures
76	
77	The following 9 tests fail independent of any recent changes (verified at commit b552cecd and later):
78	
79	- **`dup_s8_s16`, `dup_s8_s16_reordered`** — Compilation error: `test_malloc` implicit declaration (legacy test harness issue)
80	- **`mat_mul_kernel_q7`, `mat_mul_kernel_q7_2`, `mat_mul_kernel_q7_bias_2sft`, `mat_mul_kernel_q7_q15_bias_2sft`, `mat_mul_kernel_q7_q15_reordered_bias_2sft`** — Same `test_malloc` compilation error
81	- **`lstm_unidirectional_s16`, `lstm_unidirectional_s8`** — Missing golden data (`"Error on reading golden!!!"`)
82	- **`get_version_libnn`** — Reports UNKNOWN (prints version string, no accuracy check)
83	
84	### FPGA Performance Testing
85	
86	**IMPORTANT:** `test_perf.sh` links against `libnn.a` at the repo root. You must rebuild the library from source before profiling, otherwise you'll measure the toolchain's built-in (old) library.
87	
88	```bash
89	# Step 1: Rebuild libnn from source (required after any code change)
90	export PATH="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:$PATH"
91	/usr/bin/bash build.sh AX45MPV
92	
93	# Step 2a: Profile a single function
94	mkdir -p build_perf && cd build_perf
95	/usr/bin/bash ../test_perf.sh ax45mpv riscv64-elf-gcc BOARD <function_name>
96	# Example:
97	/usr/bin/bash ../test_perf.sh ax45mpv riscv64-elf-gcc BOARD conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_sym_bias_fast
98	
99	# Step 2b: Profile all functions (full suite)
100	mkdir -p build_perf && cd build_perf
101	/usr/bin/bash ../test_perf.sh ax45mpv riscv64-elf-gcc BOARD
102	```
103	
104	The script auto-configures ast530 toolchain PATH, FPGA board env vars (`MYPC`, `MYPORT`), and clears `First_Reset_done`. Output: `test_sh_<TIMESTAMP>/performance.csv` with per-function instruction and cycle counts. Single-function results are printed to stdout and `test_perf.log`.
105	
106	Baseline data: `build_perf_baseline/test_sh_20260321162450/performance.csv`
107	
108	## Architecture
109	
110	### Source Organization
111	
112	All source lives under `Source/` in 9 function categories:
113	- **ConvolutionFunctions/** — conv1x1, convHWC, depthwise conv, grouped conv, transposed conv
114	- **FullyConnectedFunctions/** — fully connected layers
115	- **ActivationFunctions/** — relu, sigmoid, tanh, silu, leaky relu, etc.
116	- **PoolingFunctions/** — average/max pooling
117	- **SoftmaxFunctions/** — softmax variants
118	- **BasicFunctions/** — elementwise add, multiply
119	- **ConcatenationFunctions/** — tensor concatenation
120	- **NNSupportFunctions/** — data conversion, requantization, buffer helpers
121	- **UtilFunctions/** — NMS, decode_dist2box, focal loss, etc.
122	
123	### Function Naming Convention
124	
125	```
126	riscv_nn_<operation>_<layout>_<input_type>_<weight_type>_<output_type>_<quant_mode>_<suffix>
127	```
128	
129	Example: `riscv_nn_conv_1x1_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_fast_any`
130	- `conv_1x1_HWC` — 1x1 convolution in HWC layout
131	- `s8_s8_s8` — signed 8-bit input, weight, output
132	- `asym` — asymmetric quantization (`sym` = symmetric, `sft` = shift-based)
133	- `bias` — includes bias addition
134	- `fast_any` — optimized variant, any channel count
135	
136	Data types: `s4`, `s8`, `s16`, `f16`, `f32`, `u8`
137	
138	### Multi-variant Implementation Pattern
139	
140	Each function has up to 3 implementation paths selected at compile time via preprocessor guards:
141	
142	1. **Vector (RVV)** — `#ifdef ENA_VEC_ISA` with version checks via `NDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION`
143	2. **DSP** — `#ifdef NDS_CPU_64` / P-extension intrinsics
144	3. **Plain C** — fallback, always present
145	
146	Vector internal headers are versioned: `internal_vec_isa_v0_8.h`, `internal_vec_isa_v1_0.h`, etc.
147	
148	### Headers
149	
150	- **Public API** (`Include/`): `riscv_nn_convolution.h`, `riscv_nn_activation.h`, `riscv_nn_types.h`, etc.
151	- **Internal** (`internal/`): `internal_nn_math.h` (math macros/inline helpers), `internal_dsp_isa.h` (DSP intrinsics), `internal_vec_*.h` (vector intrinsics)
152	
153	### CMSIS-NN Compatibility
154	
155	`cmsis_include/` provides adapter headers (`convert_between_andes_nn_and_cmsis_nn.h`) mapping ARM CMSIS-NN APIs to libnn equivalents. Supports CMSIS-NN 3.0, 3.2, and 4.1.
156	
157	## Commit Message Convention
158	
159	Prefix with category tag in brackets:
160	- `[opt/rvv]` — RISC-V vector extension optimization
161	- `[opt/dsp]` — DSP extension optimization
162	- `[bug-fix]` — Bug fix
163	- `[new fun]` — New function added
164	- `[fine-tune]` — Algorithm tuning
165	- `[misc]` — Maintenance/cleanup
166	
167	## Compilation Flags
168	
169	Key defines that control code paths:
170	- `-DENA_VEC_ISA` — Enable vector ISA code paths
171	- `-DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80|1000` — Select RVV version (0.8 or 1.0)
172	- `-DMAX_VLEN=512|1024` — Maximum vector length
173	- `-DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD` — Enable vector dot product instructions
174	- `-DENA_45_SERIES_OPT` — Auto-set for 45-series CPU tuning
175	- `-DENA_ACE_RVV` — Enable ACE (Andes Customization Engine) extensions
176	
177	Base flags: `-O3 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -Wall -Werror`
178

25	    print(f"  {GREEN}[OK]{RESET} {msg}")
26	
27	def fail(msg):
28	    print(f"  {RED}[FAIL]{RESET} {msg}")
29	
30	def warn(msg):
31	    print(f"  {YELLOW}[WARN]{RESET} {msg}")
32	
33	def header(msg):
34	    print(f"\n{BOLD}=== {msg} ==={RESET}")
35	
36	def die(msg):
37	    fail(msg)
38	    sys.exit(1)
39	
40	
41	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
42	# Step 1: Load config.toml, verify all required keys exist
43	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
44	def step_load_config():
45	    header("Step 1: Load config.toml")
46	
47	    if not os.path.isfile(CONFIG_PATH):
48	        die(f"config.toml not found at {CONFIG_PATH}")
49	
50	    ok(f"Found {CONFIG_PATH}")
51	
52	    with open(CONFIG_PATH, "rb") as f:
53	        cfg = tomllib.load(f)
54	
55	    required = {
56	        "toolchain": ["bin_dir", "prefix"],
57	        "target": ["cpu", "fpga_config"],
58	        "qemu": ["binary", "vlen", "timeout"],
59	        "fpga": ["host", "port", "timeout"],
60	        "limits": ["max_iterations", "min_improvement_pct"],
61	    }
62	
63	    for section, keys in required.items():
64	        if section not in cfg:
65	            die(f"Missing section [{section}]")
66	        for key in keys:
67	            if key not in cfg[section]:
68	                die(f"Missing key [{section}].{key}")
69	            ok(f"[{section}].{key} = {cfg[section][key]}")
70	
71	    return cfg
72	
73	
74	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
75	# Step 2: Toolchain - bin_dir exists, gcc exists, Andes flags work
76	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
77	def step_toolchain(cfg):
78	    header("Step 2: Toolchain")
79	
80	    bin_dir = cfg["toolchain"]["bin_dir"]
81	    prefix = cfg["toolchain"]["prefix"]
82	
83	    if not os.path.isdir(bin_dir):
84	        fail(f"bin_dir does not exist: {bin_dir}")
85	        # Suggest alternatives
86	        for candidate in [
87	            "/local/nick/build-ast540/bin",
88	            "/local/nick/build-system-3/toolchain/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin",
89	        ]:
90	            gcc = os.path.join(candidate, f"{prefix}gcc")
91	            if os.path.isfile(gcc):
92	                warn(f"Found alternative: {candidate}")
93	        die("Fix [toolchain].bin_dir in config.toml and rerun.")
94	
95	    ok(f"bin_dir exists: {bin_dir}")
96	
97	    gcc = os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gcc")
98	    if not os.path.isfile(gcc):
99	        die(f"gcc not found: {gcc}")
100	    ok(f"gcc: {gcc}")
101	
102	    # Version
103	    r = subprocess.run([gcc, "--version"], capture_output=True, text=True)
104	    ok(f"version: {r.stdout.splitlines()[0]}")
105	
106	    # Andes flags
107	    r = subprocess.run(
108	        [gcc, "-mtune=andes-45-series", "-mext-vector", "-x", "c",
109	         "-c", "/dev/null", "-o", "/dev/null"],
110	        capture_output=True, text=True,
111	    )
112	    if r.returncode != 0:
113	        die(f"Andes flags not supported: {r.stderr.strip()}")
114	    ok("Andes flags OK (-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector)")
115	
116	    # GDB (warn only - needed for FPGA step)
117	    gdb = None
118	    for name in [f"{prefix}gdb-nopython", f"{prefix}gdb"]:
119	        path = os.path.join(bin_dir, name)
120	        if os.path.isfile(path):
121	            gdb = path
122	            break
123	    if gdb:
124	        ok(f"GDB: {gdb}")
125	    else:
126	        warn("GDB not in toolchain (will try system GDB for FPGA step)")
127	
128	    return gcc
129	
130	
131	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
132	# Step 3: Compile hello world
133	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
134	HELLO_SRC = '#include <stdio.h>\nint main(void) { printf("HELLO_OK\\n"); return 0; }\n'
135	
136	BUILD_DIR = os.path.join(OPT_DIR, "build")
137	
138	def step_compile(cfg, gcc, tmpdir):
139	    header("Step 3: Compile hello world")
140	
141	    os.makedirs(BUILD_DIR, exist_ok=True)
142	    src = os.path.join(tmpdir, "hello.c")
143	    elf = os.path.join(BUILD_DIR, "hello.adx")
144	
145	    with open(src, "w") as f:
146	        f.write(HELLO_SRC)
147	
148	    cmd = [gcc, "-O2", "-o", elf, src,
149	           "-mtune=andes-45-series", "-mext-vector=zve64d", "-mvh", "-lm"]
150	    ok(f"cmd: {' '.join(cmd)}")
151	    r = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
152	    if r.returncode != 0:
153	        die(f"Compilation failed:\n{r.stderr}")
154	
155	    ok("Compilation succeeded")
156	
157	    r = subprocess.run(["file", elf], capture_output=True, text=True)
158	    if "RISC-V" not in r.stdout:
159	        die(f"Not a RISC-V ELF: {r.stdout.strip()}")
160	    ok(f"ELF: {r.stdout.strip()}")
161	
162	    return elf
163	
164	
165	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
166	# Step 4: QEMU - binary exists, run hello world
167	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
168	def step_qemu(cfg, elf, use_strace=False):
169	    header("Step 4: QEMU")
170	
171	    qemu_bin = cfg["qemu"]["binary"]
172	    vlen = cfg["qemu"]["vlen"]
173	
174	    if not os.path.isfile(qemu_bin):
175	        die(f"QEMU binary not found: {qemu_bin}")
176	    ok(f"Binary: {qemu_bin}")
177	
178	    r = subprocess.run([qemu_bin, "--version"], capture_output=True, text=True)
179	    ok(f"Version: {r.stdout.splitlines()[0]}")
180	
181	    qemu_cfg = os.path.join(
182	        LIBNN_ROOT, "project/ax45mpv/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg"
183	    )
184	    if not os.path.isfile(qemu_cfg):
185	        die(f"QEMU config not found: {qemu_cfg}")
186	    ok(f"Config: {qemu_cfg}")
187	
188	    # QEMU semihosting never exits after main() returns - kill after
189	    # enough time for hello world to print.
190	    hello_timeout = 10
191	    qemu_cmd = [
192	        qemu_bin, "-readconfig", qemu_cfg,
193	        "-M", "andes_ae350",
194	        "-cpu", f"andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen={vlen}",
195	        "-nographic", "-semihosting", "-bios", elf,
196	    ]
197	    ok(f"cmd: {' '.join(qemu_cmd)}")
198	
199	    if use_strace:
200	        # LLM sandbox: semihosting output goes to fd 9, not stdout.
201	        # Use strace to capture write syscalls and parse output from there.
202	        strace_log = elf + ".strace"
203	        run_cmd = ["timeout", str(hello_timeout),
204	                   "strace", "-f", "-e", "trace=write", "-o", strace_log] + qemu_cmd
205	        ok(f"Running hello.adx (VLEN={vlen}, strace, timeout={hello_timeout}s) ...")
206	        r = subprocess.run(run_cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=hello_timeout + 10)
207	
208	        if "HELLO_OK" in (r.stdout or ""):
209	            ok("Output captured via stdout: HELLO_OK")
210	            return
211	
212	        if os.path.isfile(strace_log):
213	            with open(strace_log) as f:
214	                content = f.read()
215	            if "HELLO_OK" in content:
216	                ok("Output captured via strace (semihosting fd): HELLO_OK")
217	                return
218	
219	        die("HELLO_OK not found in QEMU output (checked stdout + strace)")
220	    else:
221	        # Semihosting writes directly to the terminal, not through stdout.
222	        # Let output flow to the user's terminal; verify QEMU didn't crash.
223	        run_cmd = ["timeout", str(hello_timeout)] + qemu_cmd
224	        ok(f"Running hello.adx (VLEN={vlen}, timeout={hello_timeout}s) ...")
225	        r = subprocess.run(run_cmd, timeout=hello_timeout + 10)
226	
227	        if r.returncode == 124:
228	            # timeout killed QEMU - expected, semihosting never exits
229	            ok("QEMU ran until timeout (check terminal for HELLO_OK)")
230	            return
231	        elif r.returncode == 0:
232	            ok("QEMU exited cleanly")
233	            return
234	        else:
235	            die(f"QEMU crashed (rc={r.returncode})")
236	
237	
238	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
239	# Step 5: FPGA .inc - parse hardware features
240	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
241	def step_fpga_config(cfg):
242	    header("Step 5: FPGA .inc config")
243	
244	    path = cfg["target"]["fpga_config"]
245	    if not os.path.isabs(path):
246	        path = os.path.join(OPT_DIR, path)
247	
248	    if not os.path.isfile(path):
249	        die(f"FPGA config not found: {path}")
250	    ok(f"File: {path}")
251	
252	    with open(path) as f:
253	        content = f.read()
254	
255	    int_keys = {
256	        "NDS_VLEN": "vlen",
257	        "NDS_DLEN": "dlen",
258	        "NDS_ELEN": "elen",
259	        "NDS_DCACHE_SIZE_KB": "dcache_kb",
260	        "NDS_DCACHE_WAY": "dcache_way",
261	        "NDS_ICACHE_SIZE_KB": "icache_kb",
262	        "NDS_CACHE_LINE_SIZE": "cache_line",
263	        "NDS_L2C_CACHE_SIZE_KB": "l2_kb",
264	        "NDS_L2C_WAY": "l2_way",
265	        "NDS_L2_DATA_WIDTH": "l2_data_width",
266	        "NDS_BIU_DATA_WIDTH": "biu_data_width",
267	    }
268	
269	    # Boolean defines use "yes"/"no" strings or _INT suffix with 0/1
270	    bool_keys = {
271	        "NDS_RVV_SUPPORT": "rvv",
272	        "NDS_VECTOR_VDOT_SUPPORT": "vdot",
273	        "NDS_INT4_VECTOR_LOAD_SUPPORT": "int4_vload",
274	        "NDS_BFLOAT16_SUPPORT": "bf16",
275	        "NDS_QMAC_SUPPORT": "qmac",
276	    }
277	
278	    features = {}
279	    for vkey, jkey in int_keys.items():
280	        m = re.search(rf'`define\s+{vkey}\s+(\d+)', content)
281	        if m:
282	            features[jkey] = int(m.group(1))
283	            ok(f"{vkey} = {m.group(1)}")
284	        else:
285	            warn(f"{vkey} not found")
286	
287	    for vkey, jkey in bool_keys.items():
288	        # Try _INT variant first (numeric), then "yes"/"no" string
289	        m = re.search(rf'`define\s+{vkey}_INT\s+(\d+)', content)
290	        if m:
291	            features[jkey] = int(m.group(1)) != 0
292	        else:
293	            m = re.search(rf'`define\s+{vkey}\s+"(yes|no)"', content)
294	            if m:
295	                features[jkey] = m.group(1) == "yes"
296	            else:
297	                features[jkey] = False
298	
299	        status = "enabled" if features[jkey] else "disabled"
300	        ok(f"{vkey} = {status}")
301	
302	    return features
303	
304	
305	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
306	# Step 6: FPGA board connectivity
307	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
308	def step_fpga_connectivity(cfg):
309	    header("Step 6: FPGA board connectivity")
310	
311	    host = cfg["fpga"]["host"]
312	    port = cfg["fpga"]["port"]
313	
314	    try:
315	        sock = socket.create_connection((host, port), timeout=5)
316	        sock.close()
317	        ok(f"TCP {host}:{port} open")
318	    except (socket.timeout, ConnectionRefusedError, OSError) as e:
319	        die(f"Cannot connect to {host}:{port}: {e}")
320	
321	
322	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
323	# Step 7: Run hello world on FPGA board
324	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
325	def step_fpga_run(cfg, tmpdir):
326	    header("Step 7: Run hello world on FPGA")
327	
328	    bin_dir = cfg["toolchain"]["bin_dir"]
329	    prefix = cfg["toolchain"]["prefix"]
330	    host = cfg["fpga"]["host"]
331	    port = cfg["fpga"]["port"]
332	
333	    gcc = os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gcc")
334	    ld_script = os.path.join(LIBNN_ROOT, "project/ax45mpv/ae350-xip_hvm.ld")
335	    sim_wrapper = os.path.join(LIBNN_ROOT, "project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board")
336	
337	    if not os.path.isfile(sim_wrapper):
338	        die(f"sim-wrapper not found: {sim_wrapper}")
339	
340	    # Find a working GDB. Prefer toolchain gdb to match the toolchain.
341	    gdb = None
342	    for candidate in [
343	        os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gdb-nopython"),
344	        os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gdb"),
345	    ]:
346	        if candidate and os.path.isfile(candidate):
347	            # Quick check: can it run at all?
348	            r = subprocess.run([candidate, "--version"],
349	                               capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
350	            if r.returncode == 0:
351	                gdb = candidate
352	                break
353	    if not gdb:
354	        die("No working riscv64-elf-gdb found (needed for FPGA board runs)")
355	    ok(f"GDB: {gdb}")
356	
357	    # Compile with FPGA linker script
358	    src = os.path.join(tmpdir, "hello_fpga.c")
359	    elf = os.path.join(tmpdir, "hello_fpga.adx")
360	    with open(src, "w") as f:
361	        f.write('#include <stdio.h>\nint main(void) { printf("HELLO_FPGA_OK\\n"); return 0; }\n')
362	
363	    cmd = [gcc, "-O2", "-o", elf, src,
364	           "-mtune=andes-45-series", "-mext-vector=zve64d", "-mvh"]
365	    if os.path.isfile(ld_script):
366	        cmd.append(f"-T{ld_script}")
367	    cmd.append("-lm")
368	
369	    r = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
370	    if r.returncode != 0:
371	        die(f"FPGA compilation failed:\n{r.stderr}")
372	    ok("Compiled for FPGA")
373	
374	    # Remove First_Reset_done so wrapper resets the board
375	    first_reset = os.path.join(LIBNN_ROOT, "project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done")
376	    if os.path.isfile(first_reset):
377	        os.remove(first_reset)
378	
379	    env = os.environ.copy()
380	    env["MYPC"] = host
381	    env["MYPORT"] = str(port)
382	    env["PATH"] = f"{bin_dir}:{env.get('PATH', '')}"
383	
384	    # The expect script hardcodes riscv64-elf-gdb-nopython.
385	    # If missing, create a symlink in tmpdir and prepend to PATH.
386	    gdb_nopy = os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gdb-nopython")
387	    if not os.path.isfile(gdb_nopy):
388	        link = os.path.join(tmpdir, f"{prefix}gdb-nopython")
389	        os.symlink(gdb, link)
390	        env["PATH"] = f"{tmpdir}:{env['PATH']}"
391	        ok(f"Symlinked {prefix}gdb-nopython -> {os.path.basename(gdb)}")
392	
393	    ok(f"cmd: {sim_wrapper} {elf}")
394	    ok(f"  -> expect {os.path.join(LIBNN_ROOT, 'project/ax45mpv/gdb-ice-riscv64.exp')} {elf} ...")
395	    ok(f"  -> gdb: riscv64-elf-gdb-nopython -e {elf} -return-child-result -q")
396	    ok(f"  -> target remote {host}:{port}")
397	    ok(f"Running on FPGA ({host}:{port}, timeout=120s) ...")
398	    try:
399	        r = subprocess.run(
400	            [sim_wrapper, elf],
401	            capture_output=True, text=True,
402	            timeout=120, env=env,
403	            cwd=os.path.join(LIBNN_ROOT, "project/ax45mpv"),
404	        )
405	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
406	        die("FPGA run timed out (120s)")
407	
408	    combined = (r.stdout or "") + (r.stderr or "")
409	    if "HELLO_FPGA_OK" in combined:
410	        ok("FPGA output: HELLO_FPGA_OK")
411	        return
412	
413	    if r.returncode == 0:
414	        ok(f"FPGA returned 0 (GDB output may not be captured)")
415	        warn(f"stdout tail: {(r.stdout or '')[-200:]}")
416	        return
417	
418	    die(f"FPGA run failed (rc={r.returncode})\n  stdout: {(r.stdout or '')[-300:]}\n  stderr: {(r.stderr or '')[-300:]}")
419	
420	
421	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
422	# Main - sequential, fail-fast
423	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
424	def main():
425	    import argparse
426	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Validate config.toml step-by-step")
427	    parser.add_argument("--strace", action="store_true",
428	                        help="Use strace to capture QEMU semihosting output (for LLM sandbox)")
429	    args = parser.parse_args()
430	
431	    print(f"{BOLD}config.toml validation - sequential fail-fast{RESET}")
432	    print(f"Config: {CONFIG_PATH}")
433	    print(f"libnn:  {LIBNN_ROOT}")
434	
435	    cfg = step_load_config()
436	    gcc = step_toolchain(cfg)
437	
438	    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="validate_") as tmpdir:
439	        elf = step_compile(cfg, gcc, tmpdir)
440	        step_qemu(cfg, elf, use_strace=args.strace)
441	        step_fpga_config(cfg)
442	        step_fpga_connectivity(cfg)
443	        step_fpga_run(cfg, tmpdir)
444	
445	    header("All steps passed")
446	
447	
448	if __name__ == "__main__":
449	    main()

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Resolve a short pattern to a full libnn perf test function name.
3	
4	Usage:
5	    python3 resolve_function.py <pattern>
6	
7	Pattern is space- or underscore-separated tokens matched as substrings
8	in order. E.g. "conv_dw u8" matches "conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any".
9	
10	Exit codes:
11	    0 - exactly one match (printed to stdout)
12	    1 - zero matches
13	    2 - multiple matches (printed as numbered list to stderr, prompts user)
14	"""
15	
16	import os
17	import re
18	import sys
19	from pathlib import Path
20	
21	SCRIPT_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
22	OPT_DIR = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(SCRIPT_DIR, ".."))
23	LIBNN_ROOT = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(OPT_DIR, ".."))
24	PERF_DIR = os.path.join(LIBNN_ROOT, "Examples", "unit_performance")
25	
26	
27	def find_matches(pattern: str, perf_dir: str | Path = PERF_DIR) -> list[str]:
28	    """Return function names matching pattern.
29	
30	    Tokens in pattern (split on whitespace) become .*-joined regex,
31	    matched case-insensitively against function names extracted from
32	    t_pf_<name>.c filenames.
33	    """
34	    perf_dir = Path(perf_dir)
35	    names = sorted(
36	        f.stem.removeprefix("t_pf_")
37	        for f in perf_dir.glob("t_pf_*.c")
38	    )
39	
40	    tokens = pattern.strip().split()
41	    regex = re.compile(".*".join(re.escape(t) for t in tokens), re.IGNORECASE)
42	
43	    return [n for n in names if regex.search(n)]
44	
45	
46	def main():
47	    if len(sys.argv) < 2:
48	        print("Usage: resolve_function.py <pattern>", file=sys.stderr)
49	        sys.exit(1)
50	
51	    pattern = " ".join(sys.argv[1:])
52	    matches = find_matches(pattern)
53	
54	    if len(matches) == 0:
55	        print(f"No perf test matches for '{pattern}'.", file=sys.stderr)
56	        sys.exit(1)
57	    elif len(matches) == 1:
58	        print(matches[0])
59	        sys.exit(0)
60	    else:
61	        print(f"{len(matches)} matches for '{pattern}':", file=sys.stderr)
62	        for i, name in enumerate(matches, 1):
63	            print(f"  {i}. {name}", file=sys.stderr)
64	        sys.exit(2)
65	
66	
67	if __name__ == "__main__":
68	    main()
69

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Phase 2: Baseline measurement for libnn optimizer.
3	
4	Reads config.toml + state.json, builds unmodified libnn, runs QEMU correctness
5	check, measures FPGA performance, and outputs JSON result.
6	
7	All external tool invocations (QEMU, FPGA) go through the config-aware
8	iterate-phase scripts in opt/script/, which read toolchain paths from
9	config.toml via parse_config.sh. This avoids hardcoded path mismatches.
10	
11	Usage:
12	    python3 run_baseline.py [--skip-qemu] [--skip-fpga] [--json]
13	
14	Exit codes:
15	    0 - baseline completed successfully
16	    1 - build or correctness failure
17	    2 - FPGA perf measurement failure
18	    3 - config/state loading error
19	"""
20	
21	import json
22	import os
23	import re
24	import subprocess
25	import sys
26	import tempfile
27	import tomllib
28	
29	SCRIPT_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
30	OPT_DIR = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(SCRIPT_DIR, ".."))
31	CONFIG_PATH = os.path.join(OPT_DIR, "config.toml")
32	STATE_PATH = os.path.join(OPT_DIR, "state.json")
33	LIBNN_ROOT = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(OPT_DIR, ".."))
34	
35	GREEN = "\033[92m"
36	RED = "\033[91m"
37	RESET = "\033[0m"
38	BOLD = "\033[1m"
39	
40	
41	def ok(msg):
42	    print(f"  {GREEN}[OK]{RESET} {msg}")
43	
44	
45	def fail(msg):
46	    print(f"  {RED}[FAIL]{RESET} {msg}")
47	
48	
49	def header(msg):
50	    print(f"\n{BOLD}=== {msg} ==={RESET}")
51	
52	
53	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
54	# Loading
55	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
56	
57	def load_config(path=CONFIG_PATH):
58	    if not os.path.isfile(path):
59	        fail(f"config.toml not found: {path}")
60	        sys.exit(3)
61	    with open(path, "rb") as f:
62	        return tomllib.load(f)
63	
64	
65	def load_state(path=STATE_PATH):
66	    if not os.path.isfile(path):
67	        fail(f"state.json not found: {path}")
68	        sys.exit(3)
69	    with open(path) as f:
70	        return json.load(f)
71	
72	
73	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
74	# Toolchain environment (for build.sh only)
75	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
76	
77	_symlink_tmpdir = None
78	
79	
80	def setup_build_env(cfg):
81	    """Return an os.environ copy with toolchain PATH for build.sh."""
82	    global _symlink_tmpdir
83	    env = os.environ.copy()
84	    bin_dir = cfg["toolchain"]["bin_dir"]
85	    prefix = cfg["toolchain"]["prefix"]
86	
87	    gdb_real = os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gdb")
88	    gdb_nopython = os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gdb-nopython")
89	
90	    extra_paths = []
91	    if os.path.isfile(gdb_real) and not os.path.exists(gdb_nopython):
92	        _symlink_tmpdir = tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="baseline_gdb_")
93	        link_path = os.path.join(_symlink_tmpdir, f"{prefix}gdb-nopython")
94	        os.symlink(gdb_real, link_path)
95	        extra_paths.append(_symlink_tmpdir)
96	        ok(f"Created gdb-nopython symlink in {_symlink_tmpdir}")
97	
98	    extra_paths.append(bin_dir)
99	    env["PATH"] = ":".join(extra_paths) + ":" + env.get("PATH", "")
100	    return env
101	
102	
103	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
104	# Build
105	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
106	
107	def build_libnn(libnn_root, cpu, env, extra_flags=None):
108	    """Build libnn.a for the given CPU."""
109	    header(f"Build libnn for {cpu}")
110	
111	    cmd = ["/usr/bin/bash", os.path.join(libnn_root, "build.sh"), cpu]
112	    if extra_flags:
113	        cmd.append(extra_flags)
114	
115	    proc = subprocess.run(
116	        cmd,
117	        cwd=libnn_root,
118	        env=env,
119	        capture_output=True,
120	        text=True,
121	        timeout=300,
122	    )
123	
124	    libnn_path = os.path.join(libnn_root, "libnn.a")
125	    if proc.returncode != 0 or not os.path.isfile(libnn_path):
126	        fail(f"Build failed (exit {proc.returncode})")
127	        if proc.stderr:
128	            lines = proc.stderr.strip().split("\n")
129	            for line in lines[-20:]:
130	                print(f"    {line}")
131	        return {"success": False, "log": proc.stderr}
132	
133	    ok(f"libnn.a built: {libnn_path}")
134	    return {"success": True, "log": proc.stdout}
135	
136	
137	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
138	# QEMU correctness (via opt/script/run_qemu_test.sh --config)
139	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
140	
141	def run_qemu_test(func, vlen, timeout=300):
142	    """Run QEMU strace correctness test via config-aware script."""
143	    header(f"QEMU correctness: {func} (VLEN={vlen})")
144	
145	    script = os.path.join(SCRIPT_DIR, "run_qemu_test.sh")
146	    if not os.path.isfile(script):
147	        fail(f"run_qemu_test.sh not found: {script}")
148	        return {"pass": False, "sub_tests": 0, "log": "script not found"}
149	
150	    cmd = [
151	        "/usr/bin/bash", script, func,
152	        "--strace", "--vlen", str(vlen),
153	        "--timeout", str(timeout),
154	        "--config", CONFIG_PATH,
155	    ]
156	
157	    proc = subprocess.run(
158	        cmd,
159	        capture_output=True,
160	        text=True,
161	        timeout=timeout + 60,
162	    )
163	
164	    output = proc.stdout + proc.stderr
165	    passes = output.count("accuracy checking pass")
166	    fails = output.count("accuracy checking fail")
167	
168	    if proc.returncode != 0 or passes == 0 or fails > 0:
169	        fail(f"QEMU test failed (pass={passes}, fail={fails}, exit={proc.returncode})")
170	        if "COMPILE_ERROR" in output:
171	            fail("Compilation error")
172	        return {"pass": False, "sub_tests": passes, "log": output}
173	
174	    ok(f"QEMU passed ({passes} sub-tests)")
175	    return {"pass": True, "sub_tests": passes, "log": output}
176	
177	
178	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
179	# FPGA perf (via opt/script/run_fpga_test.sh --config)
180	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
181	
182	def run_fpga_perf(func, timeout=600):
183	    """Run FPGA perf measurement via config-aware script."""
184	    header(f"FPGA perf: {func}")
185	
186	    script = os.path.join(SCRIPT_DIR, "run_fpga_test.sh")
187	    if not os.path.isfile(script):
188	        fail(f"run_fpga_test.sh not found: {script}")
189	        return {"success": False, "cycles": 0, "instructions": 0, "log": "script not found"}
190	
191	    cmd = [
192	        "/usr/bin/bash", script, func,
193	        "--timeout", str(timeout),
194	        "--config", CONFIG_PATH,
195	    ]
196	
197	    proc = subprocess.run(
198	        cmd,
199	        capture_output=True,
200	        text=True,
201	        timeout=timeout + 60,
202	    )
203	
204	    output = proc.stdout + proc.stderr
205	
206	    inst_match = re.search(r"inst count is (\d+)", output)
207	    cycle_match = re.search(r"cycle count is (\d+)", output)
208	    accuracy_pass = "accuracy checking pass" in output and "accuracy checking fail" not in output
209	
210	    cycles = int(cycle_match.group(1)) if cycle_match else None
211	    instructions = int(inst_match.group(1)) if inst_match else None
212	
213	    if proc.returncode != 0:
214	        fail(f"FPGA perf failed (exit {proc.returncode})")
215	        lines = output.strip().split("\n")
216	        for line in lines[-10:]:
217	            print(f"    {line}")
218	        return {
219	            "success": False,
220	            "cycles": cycles or 0,
221	            "instructions": instructions or 0,
222	            "log": output,
223	        }
224	
225	    if cycles is None or instructions is None:
226	        fail("Could not parse cycles/instructions from output")
227	        return {"success": False, "cycles": 0, "instructions": 0, "log": output}
228	
229	    if not accuracy_pass:
230	        fail("FPGA accuracy check failed")
231	        return {"success": False, "cycles": cycles, "instructions": instructions, "log": output}
232	
233	    ok(f"cycles={cycles}  instructions={instructions}")
234	    return {"success": True, "cycles": cycles, "instructions": instructions, "log": output}
235	
236	
237	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
238	# Main
239	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
240	
241	def main():
242	    import argparse
243	
244	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Phase 2: Baseline measurement")
245	    parser.add_argument("--skip-qemu", action="store_true", help="Skip QEMU correctness")
246	    parser.add_argument("--skip-fpga", action="store_true", help="Skip FPGA perf")
247	    parser.add_argument("--json", action="store_true", help="Output JSON result to stdout")
248	    parser.add_argument("--extra-cflags", default=None, help="Extra CFLAGS for build")
249	    args = parser.parse_args()
250	
251	    print(f"{BOLD}libnn baseline measurement{RESET}")
252	    print(f"Config: {CONFIG_PATH}")
253	    print(f"State:  {STATE_PATH}")
254	    print(f"libnn:  {LIBNN_ROOT}")
255	
256	    cfg = load_config()
257	    state = load_state()
258	
259	    func = state.get("function")
260	    if not func:
261	        fail("No function set in state.json")
262	        sys.exit(3)
263	
264	    cpu = cfg["target"]["cpu"]
265	    vlen = cfg["qemu"]["vlen"]
266	
267	    ok(f"Function: {func}")
268	    ok(f"CPU: {cpu}, VLEN: {vlen}")
269	    ok(f"FPGA: {cfg['fpga']['host']}:{cfg['fpga']['port']}")
270	
271	    # Build env only needed for build.sh (QEMU/FPGA scripts read config.toml)
272	    build_env = setup_build_env(cfg)
273	
274	    # Step 1: Build
275	    build_result = build_libnn(LIBNN_ROOT, cpu, build_env, args.extra_cflags)
276	    if not build_result["success"]:
277	        if args.json:
278	            print(json.dumps({"status": "build_failed", "error": "build failed"}))
279	        sys.exit(1)
280	
281	    # Step 2: QEMU correctness
282	    qemu_result = {"pass": True, "sub_tests": 0, "log": "skipped"}
283	    if not args.skip_qemu:
284	        qemu_result = run_qemu_test(func, vlen)
285	        if not qemu_result["pass"]:
286	            if args.json:
287	                print(json.dumps({"status": "qemu_failed", "error": "QEMU correctness failed"}))
288	            sys.exit(1)
289	    else:
290	        ok("QEMU skipped (--skip-qemu)")
291	
292	    # Step 3: FPGA perf
293	    fpga_result = {"success": True, "cycles": 0, "instructions": 0}
294	    if not args.skip_fpga:
295	        fpga_result = run_fpga_perf(func)
296	        if not fpga_result["success"]:
297	            if args.json:
298	                print(json.dumps({"status": "fpga_failed", "error": "FPGA perf failed"}))
299	            sys.exit(2)
300	    else:
301	        ok("FPGA skipped (--skip-fpga)")
302	
303	    # Summary
304	    header("Baseline complete")
305	    result = {
306	        "status": "ok",
307	        "function": func,
308	        "cycles": fpga_result["cycles"],
309	        "instructions": fpga_result["instructions"],
310	        "qemu_sub_tests": qemu_result["sub_tests"],
311	    }
312	    ok(f"Cycles:       {result['cycles']}")
313	    ok(f"Instructions: {result['instructions']}")
314	    ok(f"CPI:          {result['cycles'] / max(result['instructions'], 1):.2f}")
315	
316	    if args.json:
317	        print(json.dumps(result))
318	
319	    print(f"\n{BOLD}=== All steps passed ==={RESET}")
320	    return result
321	
322	
323	if __name__ == "__main__":
324	    main()

1	# Implementation Plan: Autonomous Agentic libnn Optimization Workflow
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	libnn functions have multi-variant C implementations (plain C / DSP / RVV). Optimizing a function means writing a better RVV implementation for a specific FPGA target. Currently this is manual: read source, write code, build, test on QEMU, profile on FPGA, iterate.
6	
7	This plan defines how **Claude Code directly drives the optimization loop** -- no Python orchestration framework. The LLM reads source files, parses hardware configs, writes optimized C code, and calls existing shell scripts for build/test/profile. For state persistence across sessions, a simple JSON file tracks progress.
8	
9	The optimization is specialized for a **designated target configuration** (CPU, VLEN, cache hierarchy, ISA extensions) and **known static-time input shapes** (batch size, spatial dimensions, channel counts, kernel sizes, etc.) extracted from existing test/example C files. Dynamic shapes are not supported -- each optimization run targets a fixed set of input dimensions, enabling aggressive tiling, unrolling, and register allocation decisions that would not be valid for arbitrary shapes.
10	
11	**The baseline code path is never modified.** Instead, the agent edits the same source file (e.g., `/home/nick/work/libnn/Source/ConvolutionFunctions/riscv_nn_conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any.c`) and adds one or more optimized paths, each guarded by its own macro. The baseline path remains byte-for-byte intact under `#else`. A/B comparison is a rebuild with or without the relevant `-D` flag.
12	
13	Macro naming:
14	- **Single mode:** one macro per function, `ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>` (e.g. `ENA_AGENT_OPT_CONV_DW_HWC_U8_U8_U8_ASYM_BIAS_ANY`).
15	- **Model mode:** the same function can appear multiple times with different shape-sets, and each shape-set gets its own specialized path. Macros are suffixed by shape-set index: `ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>_S0`, `ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>_S1`, ... Dispatch at the top of the function picks the right specialization by comparing the incoming shape arguments against the compile-time constants of each shape-set; if none match, fall through to the baseline `#else`. The index-to-shape-set mapping is recorded in `state.json` so the build flags and dispatcher stay in sync.
16	
17	The FPGA configuration (Verilog `.inc` file like `ax45mpv_v1024_8mb_config.inc`) provides the ground truth for target features: VLEN, DLEN, L2 cache size, ISA extensions, etc. Cache sizes are especially important since the library's tiling GEMM currently hardcodes L1=512KB -- the actual L2=8MB and L1 D-cache=32KB from the config should drive tiling parameter selection.
18	
19	## Two Modes of Operation
20	
21	### Mode 1: Single Function
22	
23	**Input:** A performance test file from `Examples/unit_performance/`, e.g.:
24	```
25	Examples/unit_performance/t_pf_conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any.c
26	```
27	
28	The LLM reads this file and extracts:
29	- **Function name** from the `riscv_nn_*()` call site
30	- **All static input shapes** from the `#define` macros at the top of the file (`INPUT_X=16`, `INPUT_Y=15`, `INPUT_CH=4`, `KERNEL_X=2`, `CH_MULT=192`, `STRIDE_X=1`, `PAD_X=0`, etc.)
31	- **Quantization parameters** (`OUT_SHIFT`, `OUT_MULT`, `INPUT_OFFSET`, `FILTER_OFFSET`, `OUTPUT_OFFSET`, activation min/max)
32	- **Data types** inferred from the function name suffix (`u8_u8_u8`, `s8_s8_s8`, etc.)
33	
34	No separate TOML or config file needed -- the perf test file *is* the task specification.
35	
36	### Mode 2: Complete Model
37	
38	**Input:** A model example C file, e.g.:
39	```
40	Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/cifar10/nds_nn_examples_cifar10.c
41	```
42	
43	The LLM reads the model file and its companion parameter header (found via `#include`) to extract the full op sequence with concrete shapes:
44	
45	| # | Function | Key shapes |
46	|---|----------|-----------|
47	| 1 | `conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_RGB_sft_bias_fast` | im_dim=32, im_ch=3, ker=5, out_ch=32, stride=1, pad=2 |
48	| 2 | `relu_s8` | size=32*32*32 |
49	| 3 | `maxpool_HWC_s8` | im_dim=32, ch=32, ker=3, stride=2 |
50	| 4 | `conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast` | im_dim=16, im_ch=32, ker=5, out_ch=16 |
51	| 5 | `relu_s8` | size=16*16*16 |
52	| 6 | `maxpool_HWC_s8` | im_dim=16, ch=16, ker=3, stride=2 |
53	| 7 | `conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast` | im_dim=8, im_ch=16, ker=5, out_ch=32 |
54	| 8 | `relu_s8` | size=8*8*32 |
55	| 9 | `maxpool_HWC_s8` | im_dim=8, ch=32, ker=3, stride=2 |
56	| 10 | `fc_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast` | in_dim=512, out=10 |
57	| 11 | `softmax_s8_fast` | size=10 |
58	
59	The LLM profiles all (function, shape-set) pairs and optimizes every one of them to reach the best total model cycle count. Cost per pair is measured directly -- baseline cycles at that exact shape, multiplied by how many times the model invokes the function with that shape. Same function with two different shape sets = two independent pairs with independent costs (a 32x32 conv and an 8x8 conv share a name but not a cycle budget). Ranking by per-pair total cycles only sets work *order* so the largest wins land first and model-level cycles drop monotonically; it does not gate coverage. The long tail is still specialized -- a small kernel invoked many times can dominate once the heavy convs are tuned.
60	
61	## Directory Structure
62	
63	```
64	agentic_libnn_opt/
65	  plan.md                                   # This file
66	  opt/
67	    config.toml                             # Target config (toolchain, FPGA, QEMU paths)
68	    state.json                              # Optimization state (persists across sessions)
69	    patches/                                # Per-iteration diffs against libnn Source/ (audit trail)
70	      iter_<N>_<func>.patch
71	    log/                                    # Build/test/perf logs per iteration
72	  # The agent's macro for the new path:
73	  #   -DENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>[_S<N>]            enables the optimized code path in Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c
74	  ax45mpv_v1024_8mb_config.inc              # FPGA Verilog config (input)
75	```
76	
77	## Configuration
78	
79	Single TOML file at `opt/config.toml` -- read directly by the LLM, no schema validation layer needed:
80	
81	```toml
82	[toolchain]
83	bin_dir = "/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin"
84	prefix = "riscv64-elf-"
85	
86	[target]
87	cpu = "AX45MPV"
88	fpga_config = "ax45mpv_v1024_8mb_config.inc"
89	
90	[qemu]
91	binary = "/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64"
92	vlen = 1024
93	timeout = 120
94	
95	[fpga]
96	host = "sw-boards.andestech.com"
97	port = 1116
98	timeout = 300
99	
100	[limits]
101	max_iterations = 10
102	min_improvement_pct = 2.0
103	```
104	
105	## State File
106	
107	`opt/state.json` -- simple JSON the LLM reads/writes to track progress across sessions:
108	
109	```json
110	{
111	  "mode": "single_function",
112	  "function": "conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any",
113	  "source_input": "Examples/unit_performance/t_pf_conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any.c",
114	  "phase": "evaluate",
115	  "iteration": 3,
116	  "baseline": { "cycles": 45200, "instructions": 32100 },
117	  "best": { "cycles": 38900, "instructions": 28500, "iteration": 2, "commit": "e4f5a6b" },
118	  "history": [
119	    { "iteration": 1, "commit": "a1b2c3d", "cycles": 42000, "instructions": 30200, "accepted": true,  "notes": "unrolled inner loop 4x" },
120	    { "iteration": 2, "commit": "e4f5a6b", "cycles": 38900, "instructions": 28500, "accepted": true,  "notes": "tiled for L1 dcache 32KB" },
121	    { "iteration": 3, "commit": "9c8d7e6", "cycles": 39100, "instructions": 27800, "accepted": false, "notes": "register pressure regression" }
122	  ]
123	}
124	```
125	
126	Field semantics:
127	- `mode`: `"single_function"` or `"model"`. Drives which loop variant runs.
128	- `function`: bare function name (no `riscv_nn_` prefix). Identifies which baseline `.c` gets the new macro-guarded path.
129	- `source_input`: path to the perf test `.c` (single mode) or model `.c` (model mode). The task spec.
130	- `phase`: current loop phase (`gather`, `baseline`, `iterate`, `evaluate`, `done`). Resumed on session restart.
131	- `iteration`: count of iterations attempted so far (accepted + rejected).
132	- `baseline.cycles` / `baseline.instructions`: unmodified library measurement. Reference point for speedup.
133	- `best.cycles` / `best.instructions`: measurements from the winning iteration.
134	- `best.iteration`: which `history` entry is the winner.
135	- `best.commit`: SHA of the commit holding the winning edit of `Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c`. Required -- if the `.o` cache is evicted, check this SHA out and rebuild with `-DENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>[_S<N>]` to regenerate the exact artifact. Also the anchor for `git revert` on reject.
136	- `history[i].iteration`: monotonic integer, one per attempt.
137	- `history[i].commit`: SHA of that attempt's commit. Required for the same reason as `best.commit`.
138	- `history[i].cycles` / `instructions`: measured counts. Comparable only within the same (function, shape-set, target).
139	- `history[i].accepted`: `true` only if accuracy passed AND `cycles < best.cycles` at that moment. Rejected entries stay so future sessions don't retry the same idea.
140	- `history[i].notes`: one-line description of the idea.
141	
142	Best selection: `best = argmin(cycles)` over `history` entries with `accepted=true`; `best.commit` points at the source to restore.
143	
144	## Optimization Loop
145	
146	The LLM executes this loop directly, calling shell scripts at each mechanical step:
147	
148	### Phase 1 -- `gather`
149	
150	Parse the task spec and target features. No builds yet.

1	# Prompt: Autonomous Agentic libnn Optimization Workflow
2	
3	## Goal
4	
5	Design and implement an autonomous agentic optimization workflow for libnn.
6	
7	## Two Optimization Modes
8	
9	1. **Single function** - Optimize a single libnn function with specified input shapes.
10	2. **Model-level** - Optimize a set of libnn functions called by a given AI model, each with fixed input shapes.
11	
12	## Target Hardware
13	
14	- Optimization targets a specified FPGA configuration file (Verilog `.inc` format, e.g., `ax45mpv_v1024_8mb_config.inc`).
15	- Extract target features (VLEN, cache sizes, ISA extensions, etc.) from the FPGA config.
16	
17	## User Configuration File
18	
19	Allow the user to specify:
20	- Toolchain path
21	- Target architecture
22	- QEMU path
23	- FPGA address (domain:port)
24	
25	## Deliverables
26	
27	- `roadmap.md` - High-level vision document.
28	- `plan.md` - Step-by-step implementation plan for Claude Code, clear for both human and LLM readers.
29	
30	## Constraints
31	
32	- Each step contains exactly one feature.
33	- Each step has verifiable test cases.
34	- Each step includes: feature name, description, key files to modify, and dependencies on other steps.

150	Parse the task spec and target features. No builds yet.
151	
152	1. Read `opt/config.toml`. Resolve toolchain `PATH`, QEMU binary, FPGA `host:port`, target CPU, VLEN.
153	2. Read the FPGA `.inc` file. Extract:
154	   - `NDS_VLEN`, `NDS_DLEN`, `NDS_ELEN`
155	   - `NDS_DCACHE_SIZE_KB`, `NDS_DCACHE_WAY`, `NDS_ICACHE_SIZE_KB`, `NDS_CACHE_LINE_SIZE`
156	   - `NDS_L2C_CACHE_SIZE_KB`, `NDS_L2C_WAY`
157	   - `NDS_RVV_SUPPORT`, `NDS_VECTOR_VDOT_SUPPORT`, `NDS_INT4_VECTOR_LOAD_SUPPORT`, `NDS_BFLOAT16_SUPPORT`, `NDS_QMAC_SUPPORT`
158	   - `NDS_BIU_DATA_WIDTH`, `NDS_L2_DATA_WIDTH`
159	3. Read `state.source_input`:
160	   - **Single mode:** parse `#define` block -> shapes, quant params. Resolve target function name.
161	   - **Model mode:** parse the model `.c` and included param headers -> ordered `(function, shape-set)` list. Deduplicate on (name, shape-set).
162	4. Locate each function's baseline `.c` under `/home/nick/work/libnn/Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c`. Record path in state.
163	5. Write everything to `state.json`. Advance `phase -> "baseline"`.
164	
165	**Verifiable test:** running phase 1 on `t_pf_conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any.c` produces a state file with the exact shapes from the `#define` block and `target_features.vlen == 1024`, `target_features.dcache_kb == 32`, `target_features.l2_kb == 8192`.
166	
167	### Phase 2 -- `baseline`
168	
169	Measure unmodified library on the target.
170	
171	1. Rebuild baseline `libnn.a`:
172	   ```
173	   /usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/build.sh <cpu>
174	   ```
175	2. QEMU correctness sanity (fast, fd-9 strace mode):
176	   ```
177	   /usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/run_qemu_test.sh <test> --strace --vlen <vlen>
178	   ```
179	   Accept only `accuracy checking pass`. Failures here abort the run -- baseline is broken and no delta is meaningful.
180	3. FPGA perf measurement. For each `(function, shape-set)`:
181	   ```
182	   /usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/test_perf.sh ax45mpv riscv64-elf-gcc BOARD <function>
183	   ```
184	   Parse `performance.csv` -> `{cycles, instructions}`.
185	4. Write `baseline` and initialize empty `history`. Advance `phase -> "iterate"`.
186	
187	
188	### Phase 3 -- `iterate`
189	
190	One iteration = one candidate optimization. Loop runs until `max_iterations` or no improvement >= `min_improvement_pct` for 2 consecutive attempts.
191	
192	Per iteration:
193	
194	1. **Plan.** Inspect the current `best` source (or baseline on iter 1). Pick one concrete change (e.g. "tile K dim to fit 32KB D-cache", "LMUL=2 outer, LMUL=1 inner", "vdot accumulator 4-way unroll"). Record in `notes`.
195	2. **Edit.** Modify `Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c` in place: add the optimized path under `#ifdef ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>[_S<N>]` (replacing the prior iteration's `ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>[_S<N>]` block if present). The baseline `#else` branch remains untouched byte-for-byte.
196	3. **Build.** Rebuild `libnn.a` twice: once without `ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>[_S<N>]` (sanity -- baseline must still compile and pass) and once with `-DENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>[_S<N>]` for perf. Output goes to `opt/build/libnn.a`.
197	4. **Correctness.** Rerun QEMU strace test. On `fail` -> mark `accepted=false`, skip perf, continue.
198	5. **Perf.** Rerun `test_perf.sh` pointing at `opt/build/libnn.a`. Collect cycles/instructions.
199	6. **Commit.** `git -C /home/nick/work/libnn add Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c && git commit -m "[opt/rvv] iter N: <notes>"`. Record SHA. Also snapshot the diff to `opt/patches/iter_<N>_<func>.patch` for the audit trail.
200	7. **Accept/reject.** `accepted = pass && cycles < best.cycles`. Update `best` if accepted.
201	8. Append to `history`. Persist `state.json`.
202	
203	**Verifiable test:** after a forced-regression iteration (e.g. add a `nop` loop), `history[N].accepted == false` and `best` is unchanged.

206	
207	1. Restore `best.commit` in the libnn worktree -- its edit to `Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c` is the winning artifact.

209	3. Rerun FPGA perf one final time, confirm it matches `best.cycles` within +/- 0.5%.

211	5. Emit `opt/report.md`: per-pair baseline -> best cycles, speedup, winning commit SHA (in libnn), one-line rationale.

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	################################################################################
3	# Incrementally rebuild a single source file and update libnn.a
4	# ~100x faster than full build.sh (1 file vs ~400 files)
5	#
6	# Usage:
7	#   rebuild_single.sh <source_file.c> [--config <config.toml>] [--extra-cflags <flags>]
8	#
9	# Examples:
10	#   rebuild_single.sh Source/ConvolutionFunctions/riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_RGB_sft_bias.c
11	#   rebuild_single.sh Source/ConvolutionFunctions/riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_RGB_sft_bias.c \
12	#     --config /path/to/config.toml \
13	#     --extra-cflags "-DENA_AGENT_OPT_CONV_HWC_S8_S8_S8_RGB_SFT_BIAS"
14	#
15	# Prerequisites:
16	#   - libnn.a must already exist (run build_libnn.sh first for initial build)
17	#   - lib_objs/ directory must exist from prior build
18	################################################################################
19	set -euo pipefail
20	
21	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
22	LIB_ROOT="${LIBNN_ROOT:-/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt}"
23	
24	# Parse arguments
25	SRC_FILE=""
26	CONFIG_TOML=""
27	EXTRA_CFLAGS=""
28	
29	while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
30	    case "$1" in
31	        --config)       CONFIG_TOML="$2"; shift 2 ;;
32	        --extra-cflags) EXTRA_CFLAGS="$2"; shift 2 ;;
33	        -*)             echo "Unknown option: $1" >&2; exit 1 ;;
34	        *)              SRC_FILE="$1"; shift ;;
35	    esac
36	done
37	
38	if [ -z "${SRC_FILE}" ]; then
39	    echo "Usage: $0 <source_file.c> [--config <config.toml>] [--extra-cflags <flags>]" >&2
40	    exit 1
41	fi
42	
43	# Resolve to absolute path if relative
44	if [[ "${SRC_FILE}" != /* ]]; then
45	    SRC_FILE="${LIB_ROOT}/${SRC_FILE}"
46	fi
47	
48	if [ ! -f "${SRC_FILE}" ]; then
49	    echo "ERROR: Source file not found: ${SRC_FILE}" >&2
50	    exit 1
51	fi
52	
53	# Load config if provided, else use hardcoded defaults
54	TOOLCHAIN_BIN="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin"
55	
56	if [ -n "${CONFIG_TOML}" ]; then
57	    source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/parse_config.sh" "${CONFIG_TOML}"
58	    TOOLCHAIN_BIN="${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_BIN:-${TOOLCHAIN_BIN}}"
59	fi
60	
61	export PATH="${TOOLCHAIN_BIN}:${PATH}"
62	
63	CC="riscv64-elf-gcc"
64	AR="riscv64-elf-ar"
65	RANLIB="riscv64-elf-ranlib"
66	OBJDUMP="riscv64-elf-objdump"
67	
68	OBJ_DIR="${LIB_ROOT}/lib_objs"
69	LIBNN="${LIB_ROOT}/libnn.a"
70	
71	# Verify prerequisites
72	if [ ! -f "${LIBNN}" ]; then
73	    echo "ERROR: libnn.a not found. Run build_libnn.sh first." >&2
74	    exit 1
75	fi
76	if [ ! -d "${OBJ_DIR}" ]; then
77	    echo "ERROR: lib_objs/ not found. Run build_libnn.sh first." >&2
78	    exit 1
79	fi
80	
81	# AX45MPV build flags (must match build.sh ax45mpv config)
82	CFLAGS="-I${LIB_ROOT}/Include -I${LIB_ROOT}/internal -c"
83	CFLAGS+=" -Wall -Werror -O3 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections"
84	CFLAGS+=" -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
85	CFLAGS+=" -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024"
86	CFLAGS+=" -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD"
87	CFLAGS+=" -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
88	
89	# Detect zfh support
90	if ${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${CFLAGS} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null; then
91	    ${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${CFLAGS} - < /dev/null 2>/dev/null | grep -q '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' && CFLAGS+=" -mzfh"
92	fi
93	
94	# Append extra CFLAGS if provided
95	if [ -n "${EXTRA_CFLAGS}" ]; then
96	    CFLAGS+=" ${EXTRA_CFLAGS}"
97	fi
98	
99	# Object file name (strip path, change .c to .o)
100	OBJ_NAME="$(basename "${SRC_FILE}" .c).o"
101	OBJ_FILE="${OBJ_DIR}/${OBJ_NAME}"
102	
103	# Step 1: Recompile single file
104	echo "[rebuild] ${SRC_FILE##*/}"
105	${CC} ${CFLAGS} -o "${OBJ_FILE}" "${SRC_FILE}"
106	
107	# Step 2: Replace object in archive
108	echo "[archive] updating ${OBJ_NAME} in libnn.a"
109	${AR} rD "${LIBNN}" "${OBJ_FILE}"
110	${RANLIB} -D "${LIBNN}"
111	
112	# Step 3: Regenerate objdump
113	echo "[objdump] libnn.objdump"
114	${OBJDUMP} -S "${LIBNN}" > "${LIB_ROOT}/libnn.objdump"
115	
116	echo "[done] Rebuilt ${OBJ_NAME} in $(date +%Ts)"

1	# Optimization Report: conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_sym_bias_fast
2	
3	## Summary
4	
5	| Metric | Baseline | Optimized | Delta |
6	|--------|----------|-----------|-------|
7	| Cycles | 2,294,079 | 553,088 | **-75.9%** |
8	| Instructions | 1,698,203 | 325,140 | -80.9% |
9	| IPC | 1.35 | 0.59 | -0.76 |
10	| QEMU correctness | 3/3 pass | 3/3 pass | - |
11	
12	## Target
13	
14	- Function: `riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_sym_bias_fast`
15	- CPU: AX45MPV, VLEN=1024, 32KB D-cache, 8MB L2
16	- Perf test: 22x22x128 input, 3x3 kernel, 32 out_ch, stride=1, pad=0
17	
18	## Change
19	
20	**File:** `Source/ConvolutionFunctions/riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_sym_bias_fast.c` line 51
21	
22	**Diff:** Single preprocessor guard addition:
23	```diff
24	-#if defined(ENA_VEC_ISA) && defined(ENA_TILING)
25	+#if defined(ENA_VEC_ISA) && defined(ENA_TILING) && !defined(ENA_AGENT_OPT_CONV_HWC_S8_S8_S8_SYM_BIAS_FAST)
26	```
27	
28	**Macro:** `ENA_AGENT_OPT_CONV_HWC_S8_S8_S8_SYM_BIAS_FAST`
29	
30	## Root Cause Analysis
31	
32	The tiling matmul path (`vec_mat_mul_rvv_tiling_vq`) uses `vqmacc_vx` with LMUL=M2
33	at SEW=E8. With VLEN=1024, VLMAX=256 e8 elements. But this function's output
34	dimension (col2) is only 32 (= out_tensor_ch), meaning only 32 of 256 vector
35	lanes are active -- **12.5% vector utilization**.
36	
37	The non-tiling path uses `nn_mat_mul_kernel_q7_bias_2sft` with `vd4dots_vv` at
38	LMUL=M2 for dot-product reductions along the 1152-element im2col columns.
39	At e8/m2, VLMAX=256 elements per pass, processing the full 1152 elements in ~5
40	passes with near-100% lane utilization.
41	
42	Additionally, the tiling path has overhead from:
43	- Weight matrix transpose (nn_mat_mul_kernel_tiling_transpose_q7)
44	- Output buffer zero-initialization
45	- Separate bias + quantization post-processing loop
46	
47	The non-tiling path fuses bias addition and 2-stage shift into the matmul kernel.
48	
49	## IPC Analysis
50	
51	IPC dropped from 1.35 to 0.59 despite the massive cycle reduction. This is because
52	the non-tiling path loads the full 36KB weight matrix for each pair of output pixels
53	(200 pairs x 36KB = 7.2MB total weight traffic), and the weights exceed the 32KB
54	D-cache. The tiling path amortized weight loads across 256 pixels, but at the cost
55	of 87.5% wasted compute.
56	
57	## Rejected Iteration
58	
59	Iteration 2 attempted 4-pixel batching via `nn_mat_mul_kernel_q7_bias_2sft_unroll4`
60	to halve weight traffic. This failed correctness (2/3 tests) due to a latent bug
61	in the unroll4 function: the weight pointer `pA` advances by 1 row per iteration
62	instead of 4, causing overlapping channel computation. The bug is masked when
63	`out_tensor_ch % 4 == 0` but produces wrong results otherwise.
64	
65	## Iterations
66	
67	| Iter | Proposal | Cycles | Delta | Status |
68	|------|----------|--------|-------|--------|
69	| 1 | Bypass tiling for vd4dots path | 553,088 | -75.9% | Accepted |
70	| 2 | 4-pixel unroll4 batching | N/A | N/A | Rejected (correctness) |
71

1	---
2	name: optimize
3	description: Fully autonomous RVV optimization loop for libnn functions on AX45MPV (VLEN=1024). Runs a 7-step FSM — select, analyze, implement, verify, measure, profile, learn — then loops back to analyze with new pipeline insights. No human intervention required.
4	argument-hint: [function_name|status|reset] [algorithm|uarch]
5	disable-model-invocation: false
6	allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, Edit, Write, Agent
7	---
8	
9	# libnn RVV Optimization Skill
10	
11	Fully autonomous optimization loop. State persisted in `.claude/opt-state.json`.
12	
13	## Current State
14	
15	!`cat /home/nick/work/libnn/.claude/opt-state.json 2>/dev/null || echo '{"step":"idle","function":"","round":0,"history":[]}'`
16	
17	## Commands
18	
19	- `/optimize <function_name> [algorithm|uarch]` — Start optimizing a function (loops until flat land). `algorithm` (default) starts at algorithm-level and escalates to micro-arch when exhausted. `uarch` starts directly at micro-architecture-level, skipping algorithm-level analysis.
20	- `/optimize status` — Show current state
21	- `/optimize reset` — Reset to idle
22	
23	Arguments received: `$ARGUMENTS`
24	
25	## Finite State Machine
26	
27	```
28	idle → select → analyze ──→ implement → verify ──→ measure ──→ learn ─┐
29	                  ↑ |          ↑          |              |              |
30	                  | |          └──────────┘              │ (uarch only) |
31	                  | |       (verify fail → fix → retry)  ↓              |
32	                  | |                                  profile ─────────┘
33	                  | |                                                    |
34	                  | └→ idle (no opportunity found)                       |
35	                  |                                                      |
36	                  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
37	                    (loop with new insights + assembly analysis)
38	
39	analyze internal flow:
40	  ┌─ check optimization_level from state ──────────────────┐
41	  │                                                         │
42	  │ "algorithm" (default):        "micro-arch" (uarch arg): │
43	  │  proposer → idea → implement  proposer → idea → implement
44	  │          → no idea → idle             → no idea → idle  │
45	  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
46	
47	Exit conditions:
48	  - analyze finds no optimization opportunity at current level → idle
49	  - max 5 rounds per function → idle
50	```
51	
52	The loop is the core mechanic: after `learn`, go back to `analyze` with fresh insights from the pipeline profile and kernel assembly. Each round builds on what was learned. The `analyze` step operates at the level set by the `optimization_level` argument — `algorithm` (default) for data flow, tiling, GEMM strategy, or `micro-arch` (via `uarch` argument) for scheduling, LMUL tuning, stalls. There is no implicit escalation between levels. The verify/measure/profile loop provides the real feedback.
53	
54	---
55	
56	## Build Scripts Reference
57	
58	All scripts are at `/home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/`. Always use `/usr/bin/bash` to invoke them.

60	| Script | Purpose | Time |

114	## State: `analyze`
115	
116	Study the current RVV code, assembly, and pipeline data to form the next optimization hypothesis.
117	
118	**CRITICAL — Identify the active code path from the COMPILED BINARY, not from source `#ifdef` guards.**
119	
120	Many `#ifdef` guards depend on macros defined in internal headers (e.g., `ENA_TILING`, `ENA_FAST_ALGO` are unconditionally defined in `internal/internal_nn_math.h`), NOT just build.sh flags. **Never guess the active path from source alone.** Instead:
121	
122	1. Check the linked test binary's objdump for what functions are actually called:
123	   ```bash
124	   # Look at the perf objdump from the select step's FPGA run
125	   grep "jal.*<" qemu-validate/work/t_<test>_perf.objdump | grep -v "gdb\|nds\|start\|__" | head -20
126	   ```
127	2. Cross-reference the called sub-functions (e.g., `nn_mat_mul_kernel_tiling_q7` vs `nn_mat_mul_kernel_q7_bias_2sft`) back to the source to identify which `#if/#elif` block is active.
128	3. Read only the ACTIVE block. Modifying dead code has zero effect.
129	
130	**Key defines active for AX45MPV** (from build.sh + internal headers):
131	- `ENA_VEC_ISA` — from build.sh
132	- `NDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000` — from build.sh
133	- `ENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD` — from build.sh
134	- `ENA_TILING` — from `internal/internal_nn_math.h:2256`
135	- `ENA_FAST_ALGO` — from `internal/internal_nn_math.h`
136	- `ENA_VEC_INTRINSIC` — **NOT defined** (dead code)
137	- `ENA_VEC_ISA_ZVQMAC` — **NOT defined** (unless toolchain provides it implicitly for some files)
138	
139	### Two Optimization Levels
140	
141	The analyze step operates at two levels, tried in order:
142	
143	1. **Algorithm-level** (default) — data flow, algorithm choice, tiling/blocking, loop structure, GEMM strategy, im2col, unrolling, LMUL selection, vector register utilization (elements per iteration, register blocking)
144	2. **Micro-architecture-level** — instruction scheduling, dual-issue, VQ→VD stall reduction, VLSU prefetching, software pipelining (assumes LMUL and algorithm are fixed)
145	
146	The level is fixed at invocation time via the `uarch` argument. There is no implicit escalation from algorithm to micro-arch.
147	
148	#### Flow within analyze
149	
150	```
151	analyze entry
152	  → read optimization_level from state
153	  → if "algorithm":
154	      → launch proposer subagent (algorithm focus)
155	      → if proposer picks an idea → proceed to implement
156	      → if proposer says "no algorithm opportunity" → transition to idle
157	  → if "micro-arch":
158	      → launch proposer subagent (micro-arch focus)
159	      → if proposer picks an idea → proceed to implement
160	      → if proposer says "no micro-arch opportunity" → transition to idle
161	```
162	
163	### Algorithm-Level Analysis (optimization_level = "algorithm")
164	
165	**On first round (round=1):**
166	1. Identify the active code path using the linked binary's objdump. Read that specific block in the source.
167	2. Find the function's assembly in `libnn.objdump`:
168	   ```bash
169	   grep -A 200 '<function_name>:' /home/nick/work/libnn/libnn.objdump | head -300
170	   ```
171	3. If the function calls sub-functions (GEMM kernels, etc.), also read those sub-functions' assembly and source
172	4. Query the wiki: read `docs/wiki/index.md`, relevant `general/` pages for similar patterns, operator page if it exists
173	5. Identify bottleneck: LMUL choice, tiling, register pressure, memory access, scheduling
174	6. Form hypothesis: what to change and expected impact
175	
176	**On subsequent rounds (round>1):**
177	1. Re-read the source (it changed last round)
178	2. Re-check the objdump for the updated assembly
179	3. Review the pipeline profile from last round's `profile` step (stored in state as `pipeline_insight`)
180	4. Look for NEW bottlenecks revealed by the profile:
181	   - VQ→VD stalls → interleave scalar/vector work
182	   - Too many VW stages → reduce LMUL, increase tiling
183	   - Poor dual-issue → reorder instructions
184	   - VLSU stalls → add prefetch, align data
185	   - Register spills in assembly → reduce register pressure
186	5. Query the wiki: read `docs/wiki/index.md` and relevant `general/` pages for the specific bottleneck pattern
187	6. Form the next hypothesis based on what the profile revealed
188	
189	**Proposer output:** 1–3 ranked ideas, each with what to change, which lines, expected impact rationale, and risk assessment. Pick the top idea as the hypothesis.
190	
191	### Micro-Architecture-Level Analysis (optimization_level = "micro-arch")
192	
193	Only active when `uarch` was explicitly passed as the second argument at invocation.
194	
195	The proposer focuses on:
196	- Pipeline stalls from profile data, instruction scheduling, dual-issue opportunities
197	- VQ→VD stall reduction, VLSU prefetching, register allocation hints, loop software pipelining
198	- LMUL and algorithm are treated as fixed — do not propose LMUL changes or algorithmic restructuring
199	- Must reference specific pipeline stalls from the kanata data or assembly analysis
200	- Must consider whether optimizations survive compiler reordering at -O3
201	
202	**Proposer output:** 1–3 micro-optimizations with pipeline stage justification. Pick the top idea as the hypothesis.
203	
204	### Output and Transitions
205	
206	**Output:** Print the analysis summary and hypothesis. Do NOT wait for approval — proceed immediately.
207	
208	**Decision gate:** If no concrete optimization opportunity can be identified (assembly looks clean, pipeline has no obvious stalls, LMUL/tiling are already optimal):
209	- Transition to idle with final summary regardless of level.
210	- Do NOT force an optimization that has no clear rationale.
211	- Do NOT escalate from algorithm to micro-arch implicitly — level is fixed by the `uarch` argument.
212	
213	**Transition:** → `implement` (if opportunity found) or → `idle` (if no opportunity at either level)

217	## State: `implement`
218	
219	Apply the optimization.
220	
221	1. Modify ONLY the active code path identified during `analyze`. The active path was determined by cross-referencing the linked binary's function calls with source `#ifdef` blocks. **Do NOT guess from `#ifdef` guards alone** — many macros (e.g., `ENA_TILING`, `ENA_FAST_ALGO`) are defined in internal headers, not build.sh.
222	2. If the function calls sub-functions (e.g., a GEMM kernel), the optimization may need to be applied in the sub-function's source file, not the conv function's source.
223	3. Do NOT touch the plain C fallback or dead `#ifdef` blocks
224	4. Save a description of what was changed in state (`changes` field)
225	5. If this is round>1 and the previous round regressed, revert the regression first, then try the new approach
226	
227	**CRITICAL — Intrinsic API selection:**
228	The active `ENA_VEC_ISA` code path uses **`NDS_VEC_*` macros** (e.g., `NDS_VEC_VSETVLI`, `NDS_VEC_VLE8_V`, `NDS_VEC_VQMACC_VX`, `NDS_VEC_VNCLIP_WX`). These are defined in `internal/internal_vec_isa_v1_0.h`.
229	
230	**NEVER use `__riscv_*` C intrinsics** (e.g., `__riscv_vle8_v_i8m2`, `__riscv_vqmacc_vx_i32m8`) in `ENA_VEC_ISA` blocks. Those require `ENA_VEC_INTRINSIC` which is **NOT defined** for AX45MPV builds. Using them causes compilation failures.
231	
232	When writing new RVV code, always:
233	- Study an existing working function in the same `#ifdef` block to learn the available `NDS_VEC_*` macro names
234	- Use explicit register names (`NDS_VEC_V0`, `NDS_VEC_V8`, `NDS_VEC_V16`, etc.)
235	- Match the LMUL register grouping: M8 uses v0/v8/v16/v24, M4 uses v0/v4/v8/.., M2 uses v0/v2/v4/..
236	- For loads: `NDS_VEC_VLE8_V` (e8), `NDS_VEC_VLE16_V` (e16), `NDS_VEC_VLE32_V` (e32)
237	- For stores: `NDS_VEC_VSE8_V` (e8), `NDS_VEC_VSE16_V` (e16), `NDS_VEC_VSE32_V` (e32)
238	
239	**Transition:** → `verify`

314	## State: `profile`
315	
316	**Only runs when `optimization_level = "micro-arch"`.**  Skip entirely for `algorithm`-level optimization and go straight to `learn`.
317	
318	
319	Run vsim pipeline analysis to understand WHY performance changed.
320	
321	**Key principle:** Extract the kernel's assembly into a standalone `.S` file and run it directly in vsim. This eliminates the slow fast-forward through test setup/semihosting. The kernel starts executing at cycle 0.
322	
323	### Profiling effort scaling
324	
325	Scale profiling investment based on the magnitude of the current round's result:
326	- **Large change (>10% cycle delta):** Use Approach A (kernel extract) or B (fast-forward) — the detailed pipeline data is worth the effort since the next round builds on it.
327	- **Small change (<10% cycle delta) or neutral/regressed round:** Use Approach C (assembly-only analysis). Spending time on vsim extraction for a marginal round yields diminishing returns. Focus the analysis on understanding WHY the change was small — was it instruction-count-neutral (check `instrs_after` vs previous), or did IPC change? This informs whether the next round should target instruction reduction or pipeline efficiency.
328	
329	### Three profiling approaches
330	
331	**Approach A: Kernel assembly extract (preferred — fast, exact)**
332	
333	1. Extract the hot loop from the perf objdump (`qemu-validate/work/t_<test>_perf.objdump`) — the linked test binary, not `libnn.objdump` (which is the full library and huge)
334	2. Write a standalone `.S` file at `qemu-validate/vsim-bench/kern_<function>.S` that:
335	   - Enables mstatus.VS for the vector unit
336	   - Sets up registers to match the compiler output (pointers, strides, loop bounds)
337	   - Allocates static `.data` buffers sized for 2 loop iterations
338	   - Runs 2 full kernel iterations (e.g., 2 output pixels each doing 3x3=9 vle8+vmax)
339	   - Exits via ecall
340	3. See `qemu-validate/vsim-bench/kern_maxpool_HWC_s8.S` for a working example
341	4. Run with:
342	   ```bash
343	   /usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/run_vsim_kernel.sh \
344	     /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/vsim-bench/kern_<function>.S \
345	     --log-cycles 2000
346	   ```
347	   This compiles the `.S`, loads it into vsim at `0x10000`, and logs pipeline from cycle 0.
348	   Output: `qemu-validate/work/vsim_kern_<function>.kanata.log`
349	
350	**Approach B: Full test binary with fast-forward (fallback — when kernel is hard to isolate)**
351	
352	Use when the function's control flow is too complex to extract into standalone assembly:
353	```bash
354	# Get function address range from perf objdump
355	grep '<function_name>:' qemu-validate/work/t_<test>_perf.objdump
356	# Run vsim with fast-forward to function range
357	/usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/run_vsim_profile.sh \
358	  <test_name> <func_addr_start> <func_addr_end> --log-cycles 2000
359	```
360	**WARNING:** Fast-forward can take HOURS for test binaries with heavy semihosting I/O (e.g., conv tests with large data). **Always set a 5-minute timeout.** If vsim doesn't complete within 5 minutes, kill it and record `pipeline_insight = "vsim timed out (fast-forward too slow); analysis based on assembly only"`. Then analyze the assembly manually (instruction mix, loop structure, register pressure) instead of relying on pipeline timing.
361	
362	**Approach C: Skip profiling with assembly-only analysis (when both A and B are impractical)**
363	
364	If the kernel is too complex for standalone extraction (Approach A) and fast-forward is too slow (Approach B), perform assembly-only analysis:
365	1. Read the GEMM kernel assembly from the perf objdump
366	2. Count instruction mix: loads, vd4dots, scalar overhead, branches
367	3. Estimate compute-to-load ratio and vector utilization
368	4. Save insight based on assembly structure rather than pipeline timing
369	This is less precise but unblocks the optimization loop.
370	
371	### Parse the kanata pipeline log
372	
373	```bash
374	python3 -c "
375	log = open('/home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/work/vsim_kern_<function>.kanata.log').readlines()
376	instrs = {}
377	cycle = 0
378	for line in log:
379	    line = line.strip()
380	    if line.startswith('C\t'):
381	        cycle += int(line.split('\t')[1])
382	    elif line.startswith('I\t'):
383	        parts = line.split('\t')
384	        iid = int(parts[1])
385	        instrs[iid] = {'label': '', 'stages': {}, 'cycle_issue': cycle, 'extra': []}
386	    elif line.startswith('L\t'):
387	        parts = line.split('\t')
388	        iid = int(parts[1])
389	        if iid in instrs:
390	            lvl = int(parts[2])
391	            text = parts[3] if len(parts) > 3 else ''
392	            if lvl == 0: instrs[iid]['label'] = text.strip()
393	            else: instrs[iid]['extra'].append(text.strip())
394	    elif line.startswith('S\t'):
395	        parts = line.split('\t')
396	        iid = int(parts[1])
397	        if iid in instrs:
398	            lvl = int(parts[2])
399	            stage = parts[3] if len(parts) > 3 else ''
400	            key = f'{stage}' if lvl == 0 else f'v:{stage}'
401	            if key not in instrs[iid]['stages']:
402	                instrs[iid]['stages'][key] = cycle
403	
404	# Print ALL instructions with pipeline stages (* = vector)
405	for iid in sorted(instrs.keys()):
406	    info = instrs[iid]
407	    label = info['label']
408	    stages = info['stages']
409	    stage_str = ' '.join(f'{k}@{v}' for k,v in sorted(stages.items(), key=lambda x:x[1]))
410	    has_vec = bool(info['extra'])
411	    marker = '*' if has_vec else ' '
412	    print(f'{iid:>4} {info[\"cycle_issue\"]:>6} {marker} {label:<55} {stage_str}')
413	"
414	```
415	
416	### Analyze the pipeline
417	
418	Look at the vector instruction pipeline stages:
419	- **VQ→VD→VC→VW** is the VPU pipeline (VW has LMUL sub-stages: VW8→VW1)
420	- **Loop period** = cycle gap between successive iterations of the same instruction
421	- **VW spacing** = gap between VW sub-stages (e.g., 18 cycles = per-element latency)
422	- **VC stalls** = instruction waiting for operand from previous VW completion
423	
424	Key patterns:
425	- **vle8 VW4→VW1**: VLSU writeback, ~4 stages for M4
426	- **vwadd/vmax VW8→VW1**: VALU writeback, 8 stages for M8
427	- **vdiv VW spacing >> 1**: Each VW sub-stage takes many cycles (per-element division)
428	- **vnsra waiting for vdiv**: VW of vnsra can't start until corresponding vdiv VW finishes
429	
430	Save the pipeline insight in state (`pipeline_insight` field).
431	
432	Note: vsim uses VLEN=512. Pipeline patterns (stalls, scheduling) apply identically at VLEN=1024; only element count per instruction changes.
433	
434	**Transition:** → `learn`
